message:
feat(cli): add --info flag for system diagnostics

Add a new --info flag to display comprehensive system information for
debugging and support purposes. This provides a quick way to verify
the cass-memory installation and diagnose configuration issues.

## Usage
```bash
cm --info          # Human-readable output
cm --info --json   # Machine-readable JSON output
```

## Output Sections

### Version
Shows cass-memory version from package.json.

### Configuration
- Global config path (~/.cass-memory/config.json) and existence
- Global playbook path (~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml) with rule count
- Workspace playbook path (.cass/playbook.yaml) with rule count
- Paths displayed with ~ shorthand for readability

### Environment Variables
- OPENAI_API_KEY: Set status with masked value (first 3 + last 4 chars)
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: Set status with masked value
- CASS_HOME: Custom home directory if set
- CASS_MEMORY_VERBOSE: Verbose mode flag
- CASS_MEMORY_NO_EMOJI: No-emoji mode flag

### Dependencies
- cass CLI: Version or error message if unavailable
- Node.js: Version (from process.version)
- Bun: Version if running under Bun runtime

## Implementation Details

### src/info.ts (new file)
- InfoResult interface defines structured output
- gatherInfo() collects all system information
- maskApiKey() shows first 3 + last 4 characters for security
- getCassVersion() spawns cass --version with 3s timeout
- getBunVersion() checks process.versions.bun
- countPlaybookRules() loads and counts non-deprecated bullets
- formatInfoHuman() renders colored, aligned output
- infoCommand() orchestrates gathering and output

### src/cm.ts changes
- Add --info option to program definition
- Handle --info flag before commander parses (like --version)
- Support --json flag combination for machine-readable output
- Import infoCommand from info.ts

## Why Pre-Parse --info
Commander.js doesn't natively support async action handlers for root
options. To avoid blocking issues and provide immediate feedback,
--info is handled before commander parses, similar to how --version
is typically handled in CLIs.

## Use Cases
- Verifying installation is complete
- Debugging "cass not found" errors
- Sharing environment info in bug reports
- Checking which playbooks are active

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
